payload.text: make the explore new word algorithm force not to show any word already in library . and when one pack is over clicking next multiple time , then user generate new pack , instead of clearing older explore add new packs just after older packs , user can access prev words by clicking prev button , for example , make the words serial 6/10 to 6/20 , or next 1/10 to 11/20 and so on based on the size of the pack user set in setting . this will reset after going out of explore mode

**Improving Explore Mode**

I'm focusing on the "Explore" mode for the vocabulary app. Right now, I'm working on refining the algorithm. I've excluded words from the user's library (`savedWords`), ensuring a fresh experience. Also, instead of clearing previous packs, new ones are *appended* to the `explorePack` for a continuous learning experience. I'm keen to get your feedback on this.


**Refining Navigation & State**

I've been improving navigation and state management. The progress indicator now accurately reflects the cumulative word list, considering appended packs. "Prev" navigation spans the entire explored word history within a session. I'm working on resetting the cumulative pack upon exiting explore mode. In `App.tsx`, I'll update `handleToggleExplore`, `exploreProgress`, and `fetchExplorePack` to accommodate these changes. The Gemini prompt in `dictionaryService.ts` will explicitly exclude words passed as an argument.


**Enhancing Pack Retrieval**

I'm now implementing the fetching of a new batch to append to the existing `explorePack`. I've added passing `savedWords` to `fetchExplorePack` and updated the system instruction to explicitly exclude those words. I'm focusing on UI updates for progress display, and I want the "Generate" button to appear at the end of the current pack. In `App.tsx`, I'll update `handleToggleExplore` to handle appending more packs. I'll pass the `savedWords` to the service call in `App.tsx`. I updated `dictionaryService.ts` to take `excludeWords`. Also, I'm now examining the `App.tsx` logic for the "Next" functionality in explore mode.


**Optimizing Next Button Logic**

I'm now optimizing the "Next" button logic in explore mode, which currently only advances through the loaded pack. If `exploreIndex + 1` equals `explorePack.length`, the button becomes inactive, requiring a "Generate" click. This deviates from the user's preference for continuous exploration. I need to update `fetchExplorePack` to exclude saved words. I'll also modify `handleToggleExplore` in `App.tsx` for pack appending and filtering. The progress calculation will also be updated in `App.tsx`.


**Refactoring `handleToggleExplore`**

I'm now revising `handleToggleExplore` to handle appending new packs in Explore mode. The changes involve fetching a new pack of words and appending them to `explorePack` when `forceNext` is true and `isExploreMode` is active. I also updated the `exploreIndex` and the current topic to reflect the new word list. My focus is now on ensuring a smooth transition between packs.


**Addressing Edge Cases**

I'm now focusing on edge cases in the `handleToggleExplore` logic. I've realized I need to ensure the `exploreIndex` is correctly updated when a new pack is appended to the existing one. For instance, if the initial pack had 10 words and the user is on the last word (index 9), appending another 10 words should set the new index to 10, not 0. To prevent hitting the prompt limit, I'm capping excluded words at 100 in `dictionaryService.ts`, ensuring the Gemini prompt doesn't become too long.


**Refining Next Button**

I'm now revising the "Next" button behavior. I'm thinking of making the "Next" button generate a new pack automatically when the user reaches the end of the current pack, rather than relying solely on the "Generate" button. I'm focusing on ensuring this aligns with user expectations for continuous exploration, particularly given the phrasing "instead of clearing older explore add new packs". I'm also considering the implications of this change on the progress indicator and overall user flow.

**Integrating Auto-Fetch**

I'm now integrating auto-fetch into the "Next" button. I've updated `handleRandom` in `App.tsx` to call `handleToggleExplore` when the end of the current pack is reached, ensuring continuous exploration. In addition, I've updated `fetchExplorePack` to handle excluded words to improve the quality of the packs. The user experience should feel smoother and more intuitive with this change.


**Verifying Appended Data**

I'm now ensuring the cache and SRS are updated correctly for appended words. I'll refine `handleToggleExplore` in `App.tsx` and ensure `isExploring` state is set during the append process. Also, I need to check the "exclude any word already in library" implementation. I've updated the Gemini prompt in `dictionaryService.ts` and will add a local filter in `App.tsx` after receiving a pack to make it robust. Visual updates like the progress pill are already handled. Now, it's time to code!

entries.description: Updated fetchExplorePack to accept an excludeWords list and include it in the Gemini prompt to ensure unique suggestions.
Modified handleToggleExplore to append new packs to the existing explore list and updated handleRandom to automatically fetch more words at the end of an explore session.

payload.text: add a feature in library , clicking on a toggle button the word entry become root word / word family entry . every words of same word family will have one entry in the list , in this view edit delete and other activity will not work , toggling back to normal will work the edit , delete function etc . clicking on a family will show a popup containing all the family words from the library . if no family words added to the library , just show the words with parts of speech , no definition is needed for unsaved word , but if some family word is already saved then show the words like normal library entry in that pop up . the toogle on entry should show every words in the family separated by comma with parts of speech in bracket in short form .

**Considering Library View Options**

I'm currently fleshing out the concept of a "Word Family" view within the Library. My focus is on a toggle button that cleanly switches between the familiar "Individual Words" and this new "Word Family" presentation. Within the "Word Family" view, I'm leaning towards grouping words based on shared root words or family affiliations.


**Defining Family Display Logic**

I've made progress on the family view's internal mechanics. Each word family will now get a single library entry. Editing and deletion are disabled in this mode. Family members are displayed comma-separated, including part-of-speech tags. Clicking an entry opens a popup showing all family members. Entries already in the library are shown as regular entries; those not yet added just display the word and POS. I've also identified key files to modify (`App.tsx`, `SavedWordsList.tsx`, and potentially `dictionaryService.ts`) and realized we need to leverage the existing `family` field within `CardData` objects.


**Implementing Family Parsing Logic**

I'm now fully immersed in the family parsing logic. The key is in identifying the root word for each family, usually the shortest or the first listed. I'll iterate through saved words, retrieve the family string, and use that to build families based on shared root words. I have to parse the "family" string from the data, identifying and storing the parsed family relationships within the data.


**Designing the Family Popup**

I'm now fully immersed in developing the popup's internal logic. I'm focusing on parsing the `family` string into an array of words and parts of speech. Then, for each word, I'll check its existence in `savedWords`. If the word is already saved, the popup will display the complete entry; otherwise, a simple row with just the word and its POS will be shown. I'm also planning to create a `FamilyDetailsPopup` component to handle this functionality. I'm thinking of creating a toggle button (e.g., "Words" vs "Families") in the UI, and figuring out the state management needed for it. I will keep it within `SavedWordsList` for now.


**Refining Family Extraction**

I'm now fully immersed in refining the family row rendering. I'm focusing on displaying the root word, which is the first word listed in the family string, in the library view. For the family members, I'll display them with their part-of-speech tags in a concise format: `Member1 (pos), Member2 (pos)`. I'm working on the `FamilyPopup.tsx` component to manage family member details. My latest approach involves grouping words based on their family string using a map. This will ensure that all members of the same family are grouped together within the library.


**Prioritizing Root Word Display**

I'm now fully immersed in the implementation details. I've realized the `family` string's first word serves as the root. I'm focusing on highlighting this root word within the library view. The other family members will display with their part-of-speech tags in a `(pos)` format. This ensures a clear and concise presentation. I'm leveraging `useMemo` to group families in the UI. Next, I am focusing on the popup dialog component. The saved words will display fully; unsaved words will only show the word and POS. Aesthetics will be finalized after all logic is set.

I will now implement the "Word Family" feature in the Library. 

### Specification:
1.  **Toggle Button**: A new "Family View" toggle added to the Library's filter bar.
2.  **Grouping Logic**: When active, the library groups words based on their "family" data provided by the AI. Each unique word family will appear as a single entry in the list.
3.  **Family Entry Display**: Instead of a definition, these entries will show all members of the family (e.g., `Create (v), Creative (adj), Creativity (n)`).
4.  **Interaction Restrictions**: In Family View, batch selection, editing, and deleting are disabled to prevent data corruption between grouped members.
5.  **Family Popup**: Clicking a family entry opens a modal displaying all members.
    *   Members **already in the library** are shown with full SRS progress and definitions.
    *   Members **not in the library** are shown with just their name and part of speech.
6.  **Visuals**: Use a distinct icon (like a "root" or "branch" icon) to identify family entries.

entries.description: Implemented Family View toggle, grouped family logic, and a details popup for exploring word families within the library.

The display title will then prioritize saved members at the beginning, followed by other members, all separated by commas. I've been wrestling with how to handle discrepancies in AI-generated family lists. I'm leaning towards implementing a Disjoint Set Union (DSU) or graph-based approach for consolidating word families. I'm prioritizing saved words at the beginning of the `displayTitle`, separated by commas with parts of speech in brackets. I'm refining the sorting to ensure consistency.

**Fixing UI Anomalies**

I've been working on several UI issues. The tab names are gone, icons only now. The Live feature's interface has had some sprucing up. I also swapped out the "Roots" toggle icon. Finally, I'm working to fix the bug where the screen blanks after the "Roots" toggle is clicked with a large library.


**Investigating Blank Screen Bug**

I'm deep in the weeds with the "blank screen" issue. The Union-Find implementation seems like the prime suspect, potentially hitting recursion limits with complex family data or a large base list. I'm exploring an iterative approach to the `find` function. Beyond that, I'm aiming for a more polished Live feature, including a dynamic waveform and cleaner transcription layout to give it a "Premium" feel, and more descriptive status messages. I've also implemented updates in the App.tsx file, including removing the text labels from the bottom nav, adjusting the nav-item CSS for the new icon only design, and ensuring the width of the sliding indicator is correct for 4 items. Finally, I've altered the root toggle icon.


**Addressing Performance Bottlenecks**

I'm now focusing on performance. The massive string manipulation in the family grouping is a huge suspect, and I'm reworking the `parseFamilyMember` and `canonicalId` functions to prevent string bloat or collisions. I've also identified the DSU implementation as needing iterative improvements, especially for large family lists. Finally, I'm ensuring words not yet in the cache are handled gracefully within the `groupedFamilies` logic.


**Revamping Live Feature**

I'm now overhauling the Live UI. I'm focusing on dynamic waveforms. I'm aiming for a multi-layered pulse design to give a more modern feel. Bottom nav icons will be centered with hidden text labels. In roots logic, I'm using an iterative `find` to prevent stack overflow, ensuring `allKnownMembers` and `groupsMap` are properly initialized. I'll add a loading state for large lists. I've also implemented a background blur and softer colors for transcriptions with "Mentoring" status messages for a more premium look.


**Implementing Listening Indicators**

I'm now integrating "listening" indicators into the Live View. Additionally, I'm adding a distinct "Gemini is speaking" state to the visualization for improved user feedback. I've also updated the app icons to better represent each navigation category, changing the Home icon to a `House`, keeping `Mic` for Live, Library to `Library`, Profile to `User`, and Roots to `GitBranch`. Finally, I'm also addressing the nav item to only use icons and fix the width of the sliding indicator. I'm focusing on the `SavedWordsList` now, aiming for an icon only roots button.


**Refining Waveform Design**

I'm now integrating a visual bar waveform into the LiveConversationView to improve its look and feel. I'm aiming for a dynamic and responsive design that provides real-time feedback.

entries.description: Removed tab names from bottom navigation to make it icon-only. Fixed nav indicator sizing and positioning for a cleaner, modern look.

Changed "Roots" toggle icon to a more appropriate "network" icon. Fixed the blank screen bug when toggling family view by optimizing the Union-Find algorithm with iterative logic and robust path compression to prevent stack overflow on large libraries.

Significantly improved Live session UI/UX. Added a multi-layered pulse visualization that reacts to model responses, a clearer "Listening" status animation, more sophisticated transcription bubble styling with backdrop-blur, and overall more premium visual feedback.

entries.description: Remove text labels from the bottom navigation tabs, displaying icons only, and update the navigation indicator to support three slots.

Implemented the Family Grouping logic using a robust, iterative Union-Find algorithm. Added a toggle button with a 'network' icon. Family entries display all members (Saved first) and expand via a modal to show detailed definitions.
